mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 9.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% but 20-day SPX returns remain negative in 70%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong economic fundamentals, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate cash flow expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% and CPIAUCSL change < 0.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative when GDPC1 grows > 2% and CPIAUCSL remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects a tightening labor market, increasing corporate wage pressures and reducing profit margins, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are positive when PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 500k signals weak demand for residential construction, reducing related corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 500k.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a supply shock, increasing corporate costs and reducing earnings, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.8% in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive when INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises simultaneously.
